title: Crisis comms after data discrepancy
description: Real-world PM scenario on Crisis comms after data discrepancy with conflicting stakeholder incentives and incomplete data.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at MetricFoundry, a Series B analytics SaaS company (168 employees, $15.0M ARR). Your immediate assignment is Crisis comms after data discrepancy. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Choose the smallest plan that can prove value without increasing operational risk.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 34827
New logo adds: 4425
Activation rate: 61%
30-day retention: 19%
NPS: 12 (n=149)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 29%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 1 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 0 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Reed Brooks): "If we cannot show credible progress this quarter, procurement pushes these deals into next fiscal."
VP Engineering (Clara Gupta): "If we skip sequencing discipline, reliability debt will compound into the next release."
Head of Customer Success (Nora Shah): "Escalations spike after handoff because expectations are set too optimistically."
Commercial note: 7 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 22 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Clearwater Finance (Operations Lead): "I can defend rollout if the first-week experience is consistent across teams."
Mariner Foods (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Pinecrest Retail (Director of Product): "We do not need every advanced control on day one, we need momentum in hour one."
Data gap: limited qualitative input from accounts that never activated.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 36
Median cycle time: 15 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 68%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 1
Release rollback events last quarter: 0
Average time to resolve escalations: 50 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does it handle disagreement constructively and concretely?

rubric.name: Credibility
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 61% and retention is 19%, while NPS (12, n=149) and 22 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Reed Brooks around the 7 deal pipeline, technical caution from Clara Gupta on dependency risk (1 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Nora Shah due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 34827 active accounts, activation 61%, retention 19%, and on-time delivery 68%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
